title: Pete Hammond's Oscar Predictions: What The Academy Voted For (And Who Didn't Cast A Ballot At All)
text: This comprehensive predictions piece addresses the 98th Academy Awards, with Hammond offering his forecasts across all major categories. He contextualizes his predictions by discussing a significant new Academy rule requiring voters to attest they've watched all nominees before voting. Hammond reveals that the new viewing requirement has created unexpected complications. Some Academy members experienced technical difficulties during voting, with one member reporting their entire ballot shut down after voting in the International Feature category. The Academy's helpline reportedly fielded numerous calls from voters encountering similar issues. The piece emphasizes that this verification requirement has discouraged some long-time voters from participating. Hammond shares an anecdotal account from an Academy member stating they chose not to vote at all this year rather than cast ballots in categories where they hadn't seen all nominees. Best Picture: One Battle After Another (though Sinners poses momentum-driven competition). Best Actor: Michael B. Jordan for Sinners. Best Actress: Jessie Buckley for Hamnet. Best Director: Paul Thomas Anderson for One Battle After Another. The analysis underscores how procedural changes can create unexpected ripple effects in award voting dynamics.
